summary: Policy-backed control for whether Storage Sense can clean temporary files. Useful for users who want to keep temporary data until they clean manually, but it reduces how effective Storage Sense is at freeing space automatically.

setting.name: Storage Sense Temporary Files Cleanup
setting.casual_explanation: Storage Sense can automatically clean temporary files when Windows decides it is helpful. This policy controls that specific part of the cleanup behavior.
setting.why_people_change_it: People disable it when they want to keep temporary files around until they choose to remove them manually. Others leave it enabled because it helps reclaim storage space automatically.
setting.tradeoffs_overview: Turning this off preserves temporary files, but Storage Sense becomes less useful as an automatic cleanup tool.
setting.professional_notes: This setting maps to AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup under the StorageSense policy key. StorageSense.admx uses enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0.

allowed_values.label: Not configured
allowed_values.meaning: Windows policy does not force the temporary files cleanup behavior.

allowed_values.label: Temporary files cleanup disabled
allowed_values.meaning: Storage Sense is not allowed to clean temporary files.

allowed_values.label: Temporary files cleanup enabled
allowed_values.meaning: Storage Sense is allowed to clean temporary files.

targets.notes: This policy is only meaningful when Storage Sense itself is allowed.

app_current_implementation.notes: The current app writes AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup = 0, which matches the documented disable behavior.

windows_defaults.label: Windows policy default

states.rationale: Policy is not configured by default.

windows_defaults.notes: This record keeps the restore-default baseline as missing even though Microsoft documents the functional default as enabled.

recommended_profiles.label: Windows default
intended_for: People who want Storage Sense to work normally
avoid_for: Users who want strict manual control over temporary files

states.rationale: Leave the policy unset and keep Windows baseline behavior.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want automatic cleanup to remain available.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You do not want Windows removing temporary files.
tradeoffs: Storage Sense can keep cleaning temporary files.
No forced policy block is applied.

recommended_profiles.label: Keep temp files until manual cleanup
intended_for: Users who want manual cleanup timing
avoid_for: Users who rely on automatic cleanup to save space

states.rationale: Disable temporary files cleanup by policy.

recommended_profiles.good_when: You want Windows to stop deleting temporary files automatically.
recommended_profiles.bad_when: You want Storage Sense to reclaim space with less manual work.
tradeoffs: Temporary files may accumulate until you clean manually.

evidence.title: Microsoft Storage Policy CSP
evidence.summary: Official documentation for AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup and related Storage Sense policies.

evidence.title: Local Microsoft StorageSense.admx mapping
evidence.summary: The local ADMX file shows enabledValue 1 and disabledValue 0 for AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup.

evidence.title: Current app implementation
evidence.summary: The provider writes AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup = 0 for the current tweak.

evidence.title: Existing tweak source record
evidence.summary: The repo already tracks provenance for this tweak in the current catalog output.

decision.why: Local Microsoft Storage Sense policy files document the exact temporary-files cleanup policy surface, value semantics, and default behavior. The app writes the documented disable value.

validation_proof.exact_quote_or_path: StorageSense.admx: key="Software\Policies\Microsoft\Windows\StorageSense" valueName="AllowStorageSenseTemporaryFilesCleanup"; StorageSense.adml: Disabled: Storage Sense will not delete the userâ€™s temporary files. Users cannot enable this setting in Storage settings.
validation_proof.notes: Local official ADMX defines the exact key and value name; local ADML confirms that the disabled policy state prevents Storage Sense from deleting temporary files.
